compiled_state.title: As a hook developer, enforcement hooks are protected from accidental fail-open handler inclusion by a machine-enforced annotation-driven boundary
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Adds a `# hook-boundary: enforcement` header comment to all enforcement hooks. Adds a pre-commit check that scans for any commit where a file annotated with `# hook-boundary: enforcement` also sources `hook-error-handler.sh`, and blocks the commit. The check is annotation-driven (not list-driven) so new enforcement hooks are automatically protected when annotated.

**Why**: Enforcement hooks (review-gate.sh, pre-commit gates) MUST block on failure — they must never fail open. Accidentally sourcing the shared handler in an enforcement hook would override their blocking behavior. The annotation-driven boundary check catches this mistake at commit time.

**Scope**:
- IN: Add `# hook-boundary: enforcement` header to: `hooks/review-gate.sh`, `hooks/pre-commit-review-gate.sh`, `hooks/pre-commit-test-gate.sh`, `hooks/pre-commit-test-quality-gate.sh`, `hooks/pre-commit-ticket-gate.sh`, `hooks/review-integrity-guard.sh`; new pre-commit check script; update `.claude/dso-config.conf` or hook registry if needed
- OUT: Non-enforcement hooks; changes to how enforcement hooks actually work internally

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 6 enforcement hooks have a `# hook-boundary: enforcement` comment as a header
  ← Satisfies: "Enforcement hooks explicitly excluded with machine-enforced boundary (header convention + pre-commit check)"
- When this story is complete, a pre-commit check blocks any commit where a file containing `# hook-boundary: enforcement` also contains a `source` call to `hook-error-handler.sh` — the check is annotation-driven (scans committed files for the header annotation, does not use a hardcoded list)
  ← Satisfies: "Enforcement hooks explicitly excluded with machine-enforced boundary (header convention + pre-commit check)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for the pre-commit check

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] The pre-commit check must be annotation-driven: scan for files with `# hook-boundary: enforcement` header among committed files, then check if any of those files contain a `source` call to `hook-error-handler.sh`. A hardcoded file list would require updating the check whenever a new enforcement hook is added.
- [Correctness] The check fires at commit time, so S2/S3/S4 authors get enforcement-boundary protection during their implementation work — do not defer S5 until after S2/S3/S4 complete

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body:
STATUS:complete
Tasks: T1=22c9-2cc0 (RED test for enforcement boundary pre-commit check), T2=1028-7439 (add headers to 6 enforcement hooks + implement annotation-driven check). Dependency: T2 depends_on T1.
comments.author: Test
